candidates.readmeExcerpt:
# Karpathy-Inspired Claude Code Guidelines

> **Looking for a managed agents platform?** Check out [Multica](https://github.com/multica-ai/multica) — an open-source platform for running and managing coding agents with reusable skills.

A single `CLAUDE.md` file to improve Claude Code behavior, derived from [Andrej Karpathy's observations](https://x.com/karpathy/status/2015883857489522876) on LLM coding pitfalls.

## The Problems

From Andrej's post:

> "The models make wrong assumptions on your behalf and just run along with them without checking. They don't manage their confusion, don't seek clarifications, don't surface inconsistencies, don't present tradeoffs, don't push back when they should."

> "They really like to overcomplicate code and APIs, bloat abstractions, don't clean up dead code... implement a bloated construction over 1000 lines when 100 would do."

> "They still sometimes change/remove comments and code they don't sufficiently understand as side effects, even if orthogonal to the task."

## The Solution

Four principles in one file that directly address these issues:

| Principle | Addresses |
|-----------|-----------|
| **Think Before Coding** | Wrong assumptions

candidates.description: Ralph is an autonomous AI agent loop that runs repeatedly until all PRD items are complete.
candidates.readmeExcerpt:
# Ralph

![Ralph](ralph.webp)

Ralph is an autonomous AI agent loop that runs AI coding tools ([Amp](https://ampcode.com) or [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code)) repeatedly until all PRD items are complete. Each iteration is a fresh instance with clean context. Memory persists via git history, `progress.txt`, and `prd.json`.

Based on [Geoffrey Huntley's Ralph pattern](https://ghuntley.com/ralph/).

[Read my in-depth article on how I use Ralph](https://x.com/ryancarson/status/2008548371712135632)

## Prerequisites

- One of the following AI coding tools installed and authenticated:
  - [Amp CLI](https://ampcode.com) (default)
  - [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) (`npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`)
- `jq` installed (`brew install jq` on macOS)
- A git repository for your project

## Setup

### Option 1: Copy to your project

Copy the ralph files into your project:

```bash
# From your project root
mkdir -p scripts/ralph
cp /path/to/ralph/ralph.sh scripts/ralph/

# Copy the prompt template for your AI tool of choice:
cp /path/to/ralph/prompt.md scripts/ralph/prompt.md    # For Amp
# OR
cp /path/to/ralph/CLAUDE.md scripts

# AI Hedge Fund

This is a proof of concept for an AI-powered hedge fund.  The goal of this project is to explore the use of AI to make trading decisions.  This project is for **educational** purposes only and is not intended for real trading or investment.

This system employs several agents working together:

1. Aswath Damodaran Agent - The Dean of Valuation, focuses on story, numbers, and disciplined valuation
2. Ben Graham Agent - The godfather of value investing, only buys hidden gems with a margin of safety
3. Bill Ackman Agent - An activist investor, takes bold positions and pushes for change
4. Cathie Wood Agent - The queen of growth investing, believes in the power of innovation and disruption
5. Charlie Munger Agent - Warren Buffett's partner, only buys wonderful businesses at fair prices
6. Michael Burry Agent - The Big Short contrarian who hunts for deep value
7. Mohnish Pabrai Agent - The Dhandho investor, who looks for doubles at low risk
8. Nassim Taleb Agent - The Black Swan risk analyst, focuses on tail risk, antifragility, and asymmetric payoffs
9. Peter Lynch Agent - Practical investor who seeks "ten-baggers" in everyday businesses
10. Phil Fisher Agent - Meticul

# Action Baseline

A GitHub Action for running the ZAP [Baseline scan](https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/docker/baseline-scan/) to find vulnerabilities in your web application. 
 
The ZAP baseline action scans a target URL for vulnerabilities and maintains an issue in GitHub repository for the
identified alerts. Read the following [blog post](https://www.zaproxy.org/blog/2020-04-09-automate-security-testing-with-zap-and-github-actions) 
for additional information.

## Inputs

### `target`

**Required** The URL of the web application to be scanned. This can be either a publicly available web application or a locally
accessible URL.

### `docker_name`

**Optional** The name of the docker file to be executed. By default the action runs the stable version of ZAP. But you can 
configure the parameter to use the weekly builds.

### `rules_file_name`

**Optional** You can also specify a relative path to the rules file to ignore any alerts from the ZAP scan. Make sure to create
the rules file inside the relevant repository. The following shows a sample rules file configuration.
Make sure to checkout the repository (actions/checkout@v2) to provide the ZAP rules to the scan action.

A modular set of compiler APIs empowering the next generation of Solidity code analysis and developer tooling.
Written in Rust and distributed in multiple languages.

Slang analyzes Solidity source code and generates a rich concrete syntax tree (CST) that can be reasoned about. This is a departure from the classic approach of "black-box" compilers, which are handed the input and only their output can be observed.

# Greenbone Vulnerability Manager <!-- omit in toc -->

[![GitHub releases](https://img.shields.io/github/release/greenbone/gvmd.svg)](https://github.com/greenbone/gvmd/releases)
[![Build and Test](https://github.com/greenbone/gvmd/actions/workflows/build-and-test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/greenbone/gvmd/actions/workflows/build-and-test.yml)

The Greenbone Vulnerability Manager is the central management service between
security scanners and the user clients.

It manages the storage of any vulnerability management configurations and of the
scan results. Access to data, control commands and workflows is offered via the
XML-based Greenbone Management Protocol (GMP). Controlling scanners like
the [OPENVAS Scanner](https://github.com/greenbone/openvas-scanner)* is done via
the Open Scanner Protocol (OSP).

The Source Academy (<https://sourceacademy.org/>) is an immersive online experiential environment for learning programming. - Playground to write and test programs
- Built-in stepper, data visualizer, and environment visualizer to debug your programs
- Assessments (Missions/Quests/Contests) to solve challenging problems while learning about programming fundamentals
- Sessions for collaborative pro

candidates.description: The highest-scoring AI memory system ever benchmarked. And it's free.
candidates.readmeExcerpt:
# MemPalace ### The highest-scoring AI memory system ever benchmarked. And it's free.
Every conversation you have with an AI — every decision, every debugging session, every architecture debate — disappears when the session ends. Six months of work, gone. You start over every time. Other memory systems try to fix this by letting AI decide what's worth remembering. It extracts "user prefers Postgres" and throws away the conversation where you explained *why*. MemPalace takes a different approach: **store everything, then make it findable.** **The Palace** — Ancient Greek orators memorized entire speeches by placing ideas in rooms of an imaginary building. Walk through the building, find the idea. MemPalace applies the same principle to AI memory: your conversations are organized into wings (people and projects), halls (types of memory), and rooms (specific ideas). No AI decides what matters — you keep every word, and the structure gives you a navigable map instead of a flat search index. **Raw verbatim storage** — MemPalace stores your actual exchanges in ChromaDB without summarizatio

# Codex plugin for Claude Code

Use Codex from inside Claude Code for code reviews or to delegate tasks to Codex.

This plugin is for Claude Code users who want an easy way to start using Codex from the workflow
they already have.

<video src="./docs/plugin-demo.webm" controls muted playsinline autoplay></video>

## What You Get

- `/codex:review` for a normal read-only Codex review
- `/codex:adversarial-review` for a steerable challenge review
- `/codex:rescue`, `/codex:status`, `/codex:result`, and `/codex:cancel` to delegate work and manage background jobs

## Requirements

- **ChatGPT subscription (incl. Free) or OpenAI API key.**
  - Usage will contribute to your Codex usage limits. [Learn more](https://developers.openai.com/codex/pricing).
- **Node.js 18.18 or later**

## Install

Add the marketplace in Claude Code:

```bash
/plugin marketplace add openai/codex-plugin-cc
```

Install the plugin:

```bash
/plugin install codex@openai-codex
```

Reload plugins:

```bash
/reload-plugins
```

Then run:

```bash
/codex:setup
```

`/codex:setup` will tell you whether Codex is ready. If Codex is missing and npm is available, it can offer to install Codex for you.

candidates.description: EmDash is a full-stack TypeScript CMS based on Astro; the spiritual successor to WordPress
candidates.readmeExcerpt:
# EmDash

A full-stack TypeScript CMS built on [Astro](https://astro.build/) and [Cloudflare](https://www.cloudflare.com/). EmDash takes the ideas that made WordPress dominant -- extensibility, admin UX, a plugin ecosystem -- and rebuilds them on serverless, type-safe foundations. Plugins run in sandboxed Worker isolates, solving the fundamental security problem with WordPress's plugin architecture.

## Get Started

> [!IMPORTANT]
> EmDash depends on Dynamic Workers to run secure sandboxed plugins. Dynamic Workers are currently only available on paid accounts. [Upgrade your account](https://www.cloudflare.com/plans/developer-platform/) (starting at $5/mo) or comment out the `worker_loaders` block of your `wrangler.jsonc` configuration file to disable plugins.

```bash
npm create emdash@latest
```

Or deploy directly to your Cloudflare account:

[![Deploy to Cloudflare](https://deploy.workers.cloudflare.com/button)](https://deploy.workers.cloudflare.com/?url=https://github.com/emdash-cms/templates/tree/main/blog-cloudflare)

EmDash runs on Cloudflare (D1 + R2 + Workers) or any Node.js server with SQLite. No PHP, no separate hosting tier -- just deploy your Astro site.

## Templates

shortlisted.description: The highest-scoring AI memory system ever benchmarked. And it's free.
shortlisted.readmeExcerpt:
# MemPalace ### The highest-scoring AI memory system ever benchmarked. And it's free.
Every conversation you have with an AI — every decision, every debugging session, every architecture debate — disappears when the session ends. Six months of work, gone. You start over every time. Other memory systems try to fix this by letting AI decide what's worth remembering. It extracts "user prefers Postgres" and throws away the conversation where you explained *why*. MemPalace takes a different approach: **store everything, then make it findable.** **The Palace** — Ancient Greek orators memorized entire speeches by placing ideas in rooms of an imaginary building. Walk through the building, find the idea. MemPalace applies the same principle to AI memory: your conversations are organized into wings (people and projects), halls (types of memory), and rooms (specific ideas). No AI decides what matters — you keep every word, and the structure gives you a navigable map instead of a flat search index. **Raw verbatim storage** — MemPalace stores your actual exchanges in ChromaDB without summarizatio

shortlisted.readmeExcerpt:
![Greenbone Logo](https://www.greenbone.net/wp-content/uploads/gb_new-logo_horizontal_rgb_small.png)

# Greenbone Vulnerability Manager <!-- omit in toc -->

[![GitHub releases](https://img.shields.io/github/release/greenbone/gvmd.svg)](https://github.com/greenbone/gvmd/releases)
[![Build and Test](https://github.com/greenbone/gvmd/actions/workflows/build-and-test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/greenbone/gvmd/actions/workflows/build-and-test.yml)

The Greenbone Vulnerability Manager is the central management service between
security scanners and the user clients.

It manages the storage of any vulnerability management configurations and of the
scan results. Access to data, control commands and workflows is offered via the
XML-based Greenbone Management Protocol (GMP). Controlling scanners like
the [OPENVAS Scanner](https://github.com/greenbone/openvas-scanner)* is done via
the Open Scanner Protocol (OSP).

shortlisted.description: Use Codex from Claude Code to review code or delegate tasks.
shortlisted.readmeExcerpt:
# Codex plugin for Claude Code

Use Codex from inside Claude Code for code reviews or to delegate tasks to Codex.

This plugin is for Claude Code users who want an easy way to start using Codex from the workflow
they already have.

<video src="./docs/plugin-demo.webm" controls muted playsinline autoplay></video>

## What You Get

- `/codex:review` for a normal read-only Codex review
- `/codex:adversarial-review` for a steerable challenge review
- `/codex:rescue`, `/codex:status`, `/codex:result`, and `/codex:cancel` to delegate work and manage background jobs

## Requirements

- **ChatGPT subscription (incl. Free) or OpenAI API key.**
  - Usage will contribute to your Codex usage limits. [Learn more](https://developers.openai.com/codex/pricing).
- **Node.js 18.18 or later**

## Install

Add the marketplace in Claude Code:

```bash
/plugin marketplace add openai/codex-plugin-cc
```

Install the plugin:

```bash
/plugin install codex@openai-codex
```

Reload plugins:

```bash
/reload-plugins
```

Then run:

```bash
/codex:setup
```

`/codex:setup` will tell you whether Codex is ready. If Codex is missing and npm is available, it can offer to install Codex for you.

items.summary: 这是一个给 AI 助手使用的长期记忆系统，把完整对话原文存进 ChromaDB，并按人、项目和记忆类型组织成可导航的“记忆宫殿”。
items.whyItMatters: 它不靠模型先做摘要筛选，而是保留上下文原文再提升可找回性，适合需要跨会话持续工作的 AI 场景。
items.whyNow: 仓库刚创建不久却已获得极高关注，且最近仍在推进，正处于被广泛试用和讨论的窗口。
items.novelty: 新意在于“全量保存 + 结构化找回”而不是“让 AI 决定记什么”，把记忆系统从摘要索引改成可浏览空间。
items.trend: 最近创建来源叠加 45k star 和持续提交，说明它不是短时噱头，而是快速扩散中的新热点。

items.summary: 它把代码、文档、论文、图片甚至视频读进来，构建可查询的知识图谱，供 Claude Code、Codex、Cursor 等多种 AI 编码助手直接理解项目结构。
items.whyItMatters: 当 AI 编码开始处理大型仓库和多模态资料时，单纯向量检索不够用，知识图谱更适合回答架构关系和“为什么这样设计”。
items.whyNow: 项目刚发布就拿到高 star，并且仍在快速迭代，说明开发者对“让 agent 先读懂上下文”这件事需求很强。
items.novelty: 它把多模态输入统一转换成结构化图谱，再嵌入主流 agent 工作流，而不是做一个独立问答工具。
items.trend: 来自最近创建列表，短时间积累 26k star 且近 3 天持续更新，热度和建设速度同步上升。

items.summary: Archon 是面向 AI 编码代理的工作流引擎，可用 YAML 把规划、实现、验证、代码审查到 PR 创建这类开发流程串起来并重复执行。
items.whyItMatters: AI 编码从“临场发挥”走向可复现工程化，需要把流程显式化、可验证化；这个项目正好切中团队落地难点。
items.whyNow: 它不是新仓库，但近期重新升温并打进 Trending，说明市场开始从玩具式 agent 转向稳定交付链路。
evidence: 成熟项目近期再次升温
items.novelty: 亮点在于把 AI coding 过程做成类似 Dockerfile / CI 的声明式 harness，而不只是再包一层聊天界面。
items.trend: 成熟项目二次起势，Trending 信号配合 18k star，显示其在工程化 AI coding 方向获得持续认可。

items.summary: 这是一个 DESIGN.md 模板集合，开发者把对应文件放进项目后，可直接让编码 agent 生成贴近目标风格的页面与界面。
items.whyItMatters: 它把设计规范转成可机器消费的文本资产，降低“会写代码但做不出一致 UI”的门槛，对 AI 生成前端尤其有用。
items.whyNow: Google Stitch 带起 DESIGN.md 概念后，这类可直接拿来喂给 agent 的设计资产正迅速扩散，而该仓库热度极高。
items.novelty: 不同于传统 design system 文档，它强调可复制进仓库、直接驱动 agent 出图和出代码。
items.trend: 新仓库在短时间内冲到 49k star，且最近仍有推进，说明 DESIGN.md 已从概念快速变成开发者实践。

items.summary: EmDash 是基于 Astro 的全栈 TypeScript CMS，面向想要 WordPress 式扩展能力但运行在 Cloudflare Workers / D1 / R2 或 Node+SQLite 上的团队。
items.whyItMatters: 它尝试把 CMS 的插件生态和管理体验搬到 serverless、类型安全和沙箱执行模型上，直击传统 WordPress 插件安全与部署负担。
items.whyNow: 项目很新，但已经形成明确的产品形态并持续迭代，适合关注新一代内容平台基础设施的人深入看看。
items.novelty: 有意思之处在于用 Worker isolate 跑插件，把 CMS 扩展机制和安全边界一起重做。
items.trend: 虽 star 不如头部爆款，但作为新仓库已到 9.2k 且近 3 天持续更新，显示基础设施方向也在快速积累关注。

items.summary: 它把“从一个研究想法到生成论文”的流程自动化，围绕多代理协作、引用校验和自我演化机制来完成研究与写作。
items.whyItMatters: 相比只会写综述的 agent，这类系统开始覆盖研究流程中的论证、协作和引用验证，代表科研自动化往更完整闭环推进。
items.whyNow: 仓库发布不久就获得过万 star，说明社区正在认真关注“自动做研究”而非单点论文助手。
items.novelty: 它把研究、讨论、写作和引用核验放进同一自主系统里，并强调 self-evolving，而不是一次性生成文稿。
items.trend: 最近创建项目快速冲到 11k star，同时仍有更新，表明 AI Research 方向的自动化工作流热度正在升高。

items.summary: 这是一个带内置学习闭环的通用 AI agent，能从经验中生成和改进技能、保存知识、检索过往对话，并逐步建立跨会话的用户模型。
items.whyItMatters: 它不只是执行任务，而是尝试把“学习、记忆、技能沉淀”纳入 agent 本体，代表更长期、自适应代理的产品形态。
items.whyNow: 项目在 Trending 上升且 star 体量很大，同时近 3 天仍在推进，说明社区对可持续成长型 agent 的兴趣在增强。
items.novelty: 核心差异是把技能生成、记忆持久化和自我改进做成内建能力，而非依赖外部拼装。
items.trend: Trending 命中叠加 81k star 和持续提交，显示它已从概念演示跨入更广泛关注阶段。
